title: Packer Images
subtitle: Golden images — bake once, deploy many

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Cloud-init is cooking dinner from scratch every night. Packer is meal prepping on Sunday. You bake a custom image with everything installed, then new instances boot in seconds — fully configured, no waiting for apt install.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
HashiCorp Packer + the Linode builder (`packer-plugin-linode`):

1. Packer spins up a temporary Linode
2. Runs your provisioning scripts (shell, Ansible, etc.)
3. Creates a disk image from the configured instance
4. Saves it as a private image in your Linode account
5. Destroys the temporary instance

Result: a golden image you can launch anywhere, instantly.

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Build a Packer template that installs Nginx, Node.js, your app code, and monitoring agents. Run `packer build template.pkr.hcl`. The output is an image ID. Use that image ID in Terraform (`image = 'private/12345678'`) to launch preconfigured instances. Rebuild the image in CI weekly to pick up security patches.
"Packer bakes golden images. Terraform deploys them. New instances boot in seconds, fully configured. Rebuild weekly in CI for security patches."

quiz.question: Why use Packer instead of cloud-init for instance provisioning?

options.explain: Cloud-init is still widely used and supported. Packer is an alternative, not a replacement.

options.text: Packer bakes a pre-configured image — instances boot instantly without waiting for install scripts
options.explain: Cloud-init runs at boot (slow). Packer runs once during image creation — every subsequent boot is instant.

options.text: Packer is free, cloud-init is paid
options.explain: Both are free. The difference is when provisioning happens.

options.text: Packer works with Kubernetes, cloud-init doesn't
options.explain: Both work in different contexts. Packer is for VM images, not K8s pods.

quiz.question: How do you reference a Packer-built image in Terraform?

options.explain: Not a valid image reference format.

options.text: image = 'private/<image-id>' using the ID from Packer's output
options.explain: Packer outputs the image ID. Use it as 'private/<id>' in linode_instance.

options.text: Upload the image file to Object Storage
options.explain: Packer saves images directly to your Linode account — no manual upload.

options.text: Packer images can't be used with Terraform
options.explain: They work perfectly together — that's the standard workflow.
